evals.prompt: Build the hero cover for model-extraction-attacks. The MDX is at content/projects/model-extraction-attacks.mdx. The slug is model-extraction-attacks.
evals.notes: Golden reference — the committed cover is a live React SVG component at src/components/project/covers/model-extraction-attacks.tsx. This eval checks whether the skill follows the three-gate procedure: first proposing directions (Gate 1) before building anything. The eval run should stop after direction proposals are written — do not proceed to Gate 2 unless simulating. Check: were 3-4 directions proposed with composition details? Was a typography fallback included? Did the model NOT start writing code before proposing directions?

assertions.name: proposes_directions_before_code
assertions.description: Model proposes 3-4 visual directions in prose before writing any React/SVG code or component — output includes direction descriptions with composition concepts, key elements, and placement notes

assertions.name: typography_fallback_included
assertions.description: At least one of the proposed directions is a typography-only fallback that does not require a diagram

assertions.name: directions_are_project_specific
assertions.description: Proposed directions reference specific technical content from model-extraction-attacks (e.g., attacker-victim pipeline, query budget, coreset selection, extraction accuracy) — not generic ML concepts

assertions.name: rendering_mode_identified
assertions.description: At least one direction proposal explicitly identifies whether it calls for a live SVG component or a static asset, and explains why

assertions.name: no_code_before_direction_selected
assertions.description: No React component code or SVG markup is written in the output before the user would have selected a direction — direction proposals are prose descriptions only

evals.prompt: Build the hero cover for model-extraction-attacks using a live React SVG component. The direction is an attacker-victim pipeline diagram showing query flow: coreset selection → victim API → surrogate training. The slug is model-extraction-attacks. Write the component and register it in the cover index.
evals.notes: Focused test on live component correctness — direction is pre-selected so the model goes straight to Gate 2. Checks CSS custom properties, viewBox, aria-hidden, and registry registration.

assertions.name: no_hardcoded_colors
assertions.description: SVG component contains no hardcoded hex (#xxxxxx), rgb(), or rgba() color values — all colors use CSS custom properties (var(--...))

assertions.name: correct_viewbox
assertions.description: SVG element has viewBox="0 0 1200 675" — exactly this value

assertions.name: aria_hidden_present
assertions.description: Both the wrapper element and the <svg> element have aria-hidden="true"

assertions.name: registered_in_index
assertions.description: Component is registered in src/components/project/covers/index.ts with the slug 'model-extraction-attacks' as the key

assertions.name: single_accent_element
assertions.description: Accent color (var(--accent)) is used on at most one structural element in the base diagram — not spread across multiple nodes or connectors
